PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurie Shigekuni for and with James Hoover.

The Court stated that we are here on Mr. Hoover's petition to open probate of will and appointment of administrator, that it is looking at the documents and questions that Mr. Konick's signature looks different on the will and the acknowledgment and that it has reviewed the additional declarations. Ms. Shigekuni stated that Mr. Konick came to Mr. Hoover's office on 8/20/04 and signed the will, that he was the legal administrator and Mr. Hoover's partner for 35 years, that Cassie & Michael witnessed the will being signed but the notary public had left for the day and they were unable to have it notarized at that time, that Mr. Konick had prepared the will himself and wanted it notarized, that he came back on Monday (8/23/04) to have it notarized, he was shaky at that point but wanted to make every effort to have this done right. Mr. Hoover stated that Mr. Konick was very anal on things like this, that he thought he needed to have it notarized, that he thinks Mr. Konick's Parkinsons was more prevalent on that day and that he had requested that Mr. Hoover initial it. The Court stated that it understood putting the initials, that the Parkinson's maybe explains the difference in signature but that you usually do not see such a change in 3 days. Mr. Hoover explained that it depends on the medication and difference in times of day. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Siu. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
